paper.title: Diverse Chemo-Dynamical Properties of Nitrogen-Rich Stars Identified From Low-Resolution Spectra

review.summary: Paper identifies 138 N-rich giant stars from SDSS and LAMOST low-resolution spectra (metallicity range -3.0 < [Fe/H] < 0.0) and analyzes their chemo-dynamical properties. One LaTeX table (Table 1) lists the 138 N-rich star IDs. No machine-readable data URLs or data-availability statements found in the paper. No external data resources beyond generic survey acknowledgements (SDSS, LAMOST, Gaia).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the 138 N-rich stars (NRP) identified from SDSS and LAMOST low-resolution spectra, serving as the primary sample for chemo-dynamical analysis. These stars are used to study the origins of GC second-generation stars in the Galactic halo.

columns.meaning: Survey identifier of N-rich star, either SDSS J... or LAMOST J... format (continued from col 2 for compact 3-column layout)

internal_tables.evidence: Table 1 provides the IDs of 138 N-rich stars as stated in Section 2 (line 416-417): 'As a result, a total number of 138 N-rich stars are available for further analysis. Table 1 provides the IDs of stars that are classified as N-rich stars in our study.' The tabular has 3 columns with identical header 'ID' — the 3-column layout is for compact display only; all entries are semantically equivalent star identifiers.
internal_tables.comments: The 138 N-rich stars are split across 3 columns purely for compact formatting. The IDs are survey designations: SDSS JHHMMSS.ss+DDMMSS.s for SDSS stars and LAMOST JHHMMSS.ss+DDMMSS.s for LAMOST stars. The N-rich selection criterion is [N/Fe] >= +0.5 and 2-sigma above the mean [N/Fe] trend at given [Fe/H].

external_resources.description: JKAS journal template website URL found in LaTeX source comments — not a data resource from the paper content. The paper is published in JKAS (2023JKAS...56...59K).
